steps.title: Statement I: The Mean
steps.content: When G is formed by adding 10 to F: $$\text{mean}_G = \frac{\text{sum}_F + 10}{56}$$ All values in F are at least 150. Since 10 is far below 150, and we're also dividing by a larger denominator (56 instead of 55), the mean decreases. Therefore, Statement I is true.

steps.title: Statement II: The Median
steps.content:
When sorted, F (55 values) has median = the 28th value. When sorted, G (56 values) has median = the average of the 28th and 29th values.

The integer 10 is the smallest value (all F values are ≥ 150), so it becomes position 1 when sorted: $$\text{median}_G = \frac{\text{27th value of F} + \text{28th value of F}}{2}$$ If the 27th and 28th values are identical, then $\text{median}_G = \text{median}_F$. Since tied values are possible, the median need not decrease. Therefore, Statement II is not required.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: Only Statement I must be less for G than for F. That matches choice A — A is correct.
